Effective Date of 1992 AmendmentAmendment by effective as if included in the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation Improvement Act of 1991, , as of , except that where amendment is to any provision of law added or amended by effective after , then amendment by effective on effective date of amendment by , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 102–550Pub. L. 102–242Pub. L. 102–242Pub. L. 102–550Pub. L. 102–242section 1609 of Pub. L. 102–550section 191 of this titleDec. 19, 1991Dec. 19, 1992
Effective DateSection effective 1 year after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1991 Amendment note under .Dec. 19, 1991section 131(f) of Pub. L. 102–242section 1464 of this title

Regulations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 102–242, title I, § 131(b)105 Stat. 2266Dec. 19, 1991“Each appropriate Federal banking agency (as defined in section 3 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act ()) (and the Corporation, acting in the Corporation’s capacity as insurer of depository institutions under that Act [ et seq.]) shall, after notice and opportunity for comment, promulgate final regulations under section 38 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [] (as added by subsection (a)) not later than 9 months after the date of enactment of this Act [], and those regulations shall become effective not later than 1 year after that date of enactment.”12 U.S.C. 181312 U.S.C. 181112 U.S.C. 1831oDec. 19, 1991

Supplementary Leverage Ratio for Custodial Banks, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–174, title IV, § 402132 Stat. 1359May 24, 2018“(a).—DefinitionIn this section, the term ‘custodial bank’ means any depository institution holding company predominantly engaged in custody, safekeeping, and asset servicing activities, including any insured depository institution subsidiary of such a holding company.“(b)Regulations.—“(1).—DefinitionIn this subsection, the term ‘central bank’ means—“(A)the Federal Reserve System;“(B)the European Central Bank; and“(C)central banks of member countries of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development, if—“(i)the member country has been assigned a zero percent risk weight under sections 3.32, 217.32, and 324.32 of title 12, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation; and“(ii)the sovereign debt of such member country is not in default or has not been in default during the previous 5 years.“(2).—RegulationsThe appropriate Federal banking agencies shall promulgate regulations to amend sections 3.10, 217.10, and 324.10 of title 12, Code of Federal Regulations, to specify that—“(A)subject to subparagraph (B), funds of a custodial bank that are deposited with a central bank shall not be taken into account when calculating the supplementary leverage ratio as applied to the custodial bank; and“(B)with respect to the funds described in subparagraph (A), any amount that exceeds the total value of deposits of the custodial bank that are linked to fiduciary or custodial and safekeeping accounts shall be taken into account when calculating the supplementary leverage ratio as applied to the custodial bank.“(c).—Rule of ConstructionNothing in subsection (b) shall be construed to limit the authority of the appropriate Federal banking agencies to tailor or adjust the supplementary leverage ratio or any other leverage ratio for any company that is not a custodial bank.”[For definitions of terms used in , set out above, see , set out as a Definitions note under .]section 402 of Pub. L. 115–174section 2 of Pub. L. 115–174section 5365 of this title

Deposit of Insurance Proceeds, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–18, title V, § 50003111 Stat. 211June 12, 1997“(a).—In GeneralThe appropriate Federal banking agency may, by order, permit an insured depository institution to subtract from the institution’s total assets, in calculating compliance with the leverage limit prescribed under section 38 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [], an amount not exceeding the qualifying amount attributable to insurance proceeds, if the agency determines that—12 U.S.C. 1831o“(1)the institution—“(A)had its principal place of business within an area in which the President, pursuant to section 401 of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act [], has determined, on or after , that a major disaster exists, or within an area determined to be eligible for disaster relief under other Federal law by reason of damage related to the 1997 flooding of the Red River of the North, the Minnesota River, and the tributaries of such rivers, on the day before the date of any such determination;42 U.S.C. 5170February 28, 1997“(B)derives more than 60 percent of its total deposits from persons who normally reside within, or whose principal place of business is normally within, areas of intense devastation caused by the major disaster;“(C)was adequately capitalized (as defined in section 38 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act) before the major disaster; and“(D)has an acceptable plan for managing the increase in its total assets and total deposits; and“(2)the subtraction is consistent with the purpose of section 38 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act.“(b).—Time Limit on ExceptionsAny exception made under this section shall expire not later than .February 28, 1999“(c).—DefinitionsFor purposes of this section:“(1).—Appropriate federal banking agencyThe term ‘appropriate Federal banking agency’ has the same meaning as in section 3 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [].12 U.S.C. 1813“(2).—Insured depository institutionThe term ‘insured depository institution’ has the same meaning as in section 3 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act.“(3).—Leverage limitThe term ‘leverage limit’ has the same meaning as in section 38 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [].12 U.S.C. 1831o“(4).—Qualifying amount attributable to insurance proceedsThe term ‘qualifying amount attributable to insurance proceeds’ means the amount (if any) by which the institution’s total assets exceed the institution’s average total assets during the calendar quarter ending before the date of any determination referred to in subsection (a)(1)(A), because of the deposit of insurance payments or governmental assistance made with respect to damage caused by, or other costs resulting from, the major disaster.”Similar provisions were contained in the following prior acts:, , .Pub. L. 103–76, § 3107 Stat. 753Aug. 12, 1993, , .Pub. L. 102–485, § 4106 Stat. 2772Oct. 23, 1992

Transition Rule Regarding Current Directors and Senior Executive Officers, , , provided that:Pub. L. 102–242, title I, § 131(e)105 Stat. 2267Dec. 19, 1991“(1).—Dismissal from officeSection 38(f)(2)(F)(ii) of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [(f)(2)(F)(ii)] (as added by subsection (a)) shall not apply with respect to—12 U.S.C. 1831o“(A)any director whose current term as a director commenced on or before the date of enactment of this Act [] and has not been extended—Dec. 19, 1991“(i)after that date of enactment, or“(ii)to evade section 38(f)(2)(F)(ii); or“(B)any senior executive officer who accepted employment in his or her current position on or before the date of enactment of this Act and whose contract of employment has not been renewed or renegotiated—“(i)after that date of enactment, or“(ii)to evade section 38(f)(2)(F)(ii).“(2).—Restricting compensationSection 38(f)(4) of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [(f)(4)] (as added by subsection (a)) shall not apply with respect to any senior executive officer who accepted employment in his or her current position on or before the date of enactment of this Act [] and whose contract of employment has not been renewed or renegotiated—12 U.S.C. 1831oDec. 19, 1991“(A)after that date of enactment, or“(B)to evade section 38(f)(4).”

children.heading: Resolving problems to protect Deposit Insurance Fund
children.label: a Resolving problems to protect Deposit Insurance Fund

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 1 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this section is to resolve the problems of insured depository institutions at the least possible long-term loss to the Deposit Insurance Fund.

children.heading: Prompt corrective action required
children.label: 2 Prompt corrective action required
children.text: Each appropriate Federal banking agency and the Corporation (acting in the Corporation’s capacity as the insurer of depository institutions under this chapter) shall carry out the purpose of this section by taking prompt corrective action to resolve the problems of insured depository institutions.

children.heading: Well capitalized
children.label: A Well capitalized
children.text: An insured depository institution is “well capitalized” if it significantly exceeds the required minimum level for each relevant capital measure.

children.heading: Adequately capitalized
children.label: B Adequately capitalized
children.text: An insured depository institution is “adequately capitalized” if it meets the required minimum level for each relevant capital measure.

children.heading: Undercapitalized
children.label: C Undercapitalized
children.text: An insured depository institution is “undercapitalized” if it fails to meet the required minimum level for any relevant capital measure.

children.heading: Significantly undercapitalized
children.label: D Significantly undercapitalized
children.text: An insured depository institution is “significantly undercapitalized” if it is significantly below the required minimum level for any relevant capital measure.

children.heading: Critically undercapitalized
children.label: E Critically undercapitalized
children.text: An insured depository institution is “critically undercapitalized” if it fails to meet any level specified under subsection (c)(3)(A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The “average” of an accounting item (such as total assets or tangible equity) during a given period means the sum of that item at the close of business on each business day during that period divided by the total number of business days in that period.

children.heading: Agency may permit weekly averaging for certain institutions
children.label: ii Agency may permit weekly averaging for certain institutions
children.text: In the case of insured depository institutions that have total assets of less than $300,000,000 and normally file reports of condition reflecting weekly (rather than daily) averages of accounting items, the appropriate Federal banking agency may provide that the “average” of an accounting item during a given period means the sum of that item at the close of business on the relevant business day each week during that period divided by the total number of weeks in that period.

children.label: I
children.text: any dividend consisting only of shares of the institution or company or rights to purchase such shares; or

children.label: II
children.text: any amount paid on the deposits of a mutual or cooperative institution that the appropriate Federal banking agency determines is not a distribution for purposes of this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: a payment by an insured depository institution or company to repurchase, redeem, retire, or otherwise acquire any of its shares or other ownership interests, including any extension of credit to finance an affiliated company’s acquisition of those shares or interests; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a transaction that the appropriate Federal banking agency or the Corporation determines, by order or regulation, to be in substance a distribution of capital to the owners of the insured depository institution or company.

children.heading: Capital restoration plan
children.label: C Capital restoration plan
children.text: The term “capital restoration plan” means a plan submitted under subsection (e)(2).

children.heading: Company
children.label: D Company
children.text: The term “company” has the same meaning as in .section 1841 of this title

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: E Compensation
children.text: The term “compensation” includes any payment of money or provision of any other thing of value in consideration of employment.

children.heading: Relevant capital measure
children.label: F Relevant capital measure
children.text: The term “relevant capital measure” means the measures described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Required minimum level
children.label: G Required minimum level
children.text: The term “required minimum level” means, with respect to each relevant capital measure, the minimum acceptable capital level specified by the appropriate Federal banking agency by regulation.

children.heading: Senior executive officer
children.label: H Senior executive officer
children.text: The term “senior executive officer” has the same meaning as the term “executive officer” in .section 375b of this title

children.heading: Subordinated debt
children.label: I Subordinated debt
children.text: The term “subordinated debt” means debt subordinated to the claims of general creditors.

children.label: i
children.text: a leverage limit; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a risk-based capital requirement.

children.label: i
children.text: establish any additional relevant capital measures to carry out the purpose of this section; or

children.label: ii
children.text: rescind any relevant capital measure required under subparagraph (A) upon determining (with the concurrence of the other Federal banking agencies) that the measure is no longer an appropriate means for carrying out the purpose of this section.

children.heading: Capital categories generally
children.label: 2 Capital categories generally
children.text: Each appropriate Federal banking agency shall, by regulation, specify for each relevant capital measure the levels at which an insured depository institution is well capitalized, adequately capitalized, undercapitalized, and significantly undercapitalized.

children.heading: Leverage limit
children.label: i Leverage limit
children.text: Each appropriate Federal banking agency shall, by regulation, in consultation with the Corporation, specify the ratio of tangible equity to total assets at which an insured depository institution is critically undercapitalized.

children.heading: Other relevant capital measures
children.label: ii Other relevant capital measures
children.text: The agency may, by regulation, specify for 1 or more other relevant capital measures, the level at which an insured depository institution is critically undercapitalized.

children.label: i
children.text: not less than 2 percent of total assets; and

children.label: ii
children.text: except as provided in clause (i), not more than 65 percent of the required minimum level of capital under the leverage limit.

children.heading: FDIC’s concurrence required
children.label: C FDIC’s concurrence required
children.text: The appropriate Federal banking agency shall not, without the concurrence of the Corporation, specify a level under subparagraph (A)(i) lower than that specified by the Corporation for State nonmember insured banks.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to all institutions
children.label: d Provisions applicable to all institutions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An insured depository institution shall make no capital distribution if, after making the distribution, the institution would be undercapitalized.

children.label: i
children.text: is made in connection with the issuance of additional shares or obligations of the institution in at least an equivalent amount; and

children.label: ii
children.text: will reduce the institution’s financial obligations or otherwise improve the institution’s financial condition.

children.heading: Management fees restricted
children.label: 2 Management fees restricted
children.text: An insured depository institution shall pay no management fee to any person having control of that institution if, after making the payment, the institution would be undercapitalized.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to undercapitalized institutions
children.label: e Provisions applicable to undercapitalized institutions
A
children.text: closely monitor the condition of any undercapitalized insured depository institution;

children.label: B
children.text: closely monitor compliance with capital restoration plans, restrictions, and requirements imposed under this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: periodically review the plan, restrictions, and requirements applicable to any undercapitalized insured depository institution to determine whether the plan, restrictions, and requirements are achieving the purpose of this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any undercapitalized insured depository institution shall submit an acceptable capital restoration plan to the appropriate Federal banking agency within the time allowed by the agency under subparagraph (D).

children.label: I
children.text: the steps the insured depository institution will take to become adequately capitalized;

children.label: II
children.text: the levels of capital to be attained during each year in which the plan will be in effect;

children.label: III
children.text: how the institution will comply with the restrictions or requirements then in effect under this section; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the types and levels of activities in which the institution will engage; and

children.label: ii
children.text: contain such other information as the appropriate Federal banking agency may require.

children.label: I
children.text: complies with subparagraph (B);

children.label: II
children.text: is based on realistic assumptions, and is likely to succeed in restoring the institution’s capital; and

children.label: III
children.text: would not appreciably increase the risk (including credit risk, interest-rate risk, and other types of risk) to which the institution is exposed; and

children.label: I
children.text: guaranteed that the institution will comply with the plan until the institution has been adequately capitalized on average during each of 4 consecutive calendar quarters; and

children.label: II
children.text: provided appropriate assurances of performance.

children.heading: Deadlines for submission and review of plans
children.label: D Deadlines for submission and review of plans
i
children.text: provide insured depository institutions with reasonable time to submit capital restoration plans, and generally require an institution to submit a plan not later than 45 days after the institution becomes undercapitalized;

children.label: ii
children.text: require the agency to act on capital restoration plans expeditiously, and generally not later than 60 days after the plan is submitted; and

children.label: iii
children.text: require the agency to submit a copy of any plan approved by the agency to the Corporation before the end of the 45-day period beginning on the date such approval is granted.

children.label: I
children.text: an amount equal to 5 percent of the institution’s total assets at the time the institution became undercapitalized; or

children.label: II
children.text: the amount which is necessary (or would have been necessary) to bring the institution into compliance with all capital standards applicable with respect to such institution as of the time the institution fails to comply with a plan under this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: requiring any company not having control of an undercapitalized insured depository institution to guarantee, or otherwise be liable on, a capital restoration plan;

children.label: II
children.text: requiring any person other than an insured depository institution to submit a capital restoration plan; or

children.label: III
children.text: affecting compliance by brokers, dealers, government securities brokers, and government securities dealers with the financial responsibility requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 [ et seq.] and regulations and orders thereunder.15 U.S.C. 78a

children.label: A
children.text: the appropriate Federal banking agency has accepted the institution’s capital restoration plan;

children.label: B
children.text: any increase in total assets is consistent with the plan; and

children.label: C
children.text: the institution’s ratio of tangible equity to assets increases during the calendar quarter at a rate sufficient to enable the institution to become adequately capitalized within a reasonable time.

children.heading: Prior approval required for acquisitions, branching, and new lines of business
children.label: 4 Prior approval required for acquisitions, branching, and new lines of business
A
children.text: the appropriate Federal banking agency has accepted the insured depository institution’s capital restoration plan, the institution is implementing the plan, and the agency determines that the proposed action is consistent with and will further the achievement of the plan; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Board of Directors determines that the proposed action will further the purpose of this section.

children.heading: Discretionary safeguards
children.label: 5 Discretionary safeguards
children.text: The appropriate Federal banking agency may, with respect to any undercapitalized insured depository institution, take actions described in any subparagraph of subsection (f)(2) if the agency determines that those actions are necessary to carry out the purpose of this section.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to significantly undercapitalized institutions and undercapitalized institutions that fail to submit and implement capital restoration plans
children.label: f Provisions applicable to significantly undercapitalized institutions and undercapitalized institutions that fail to submit and implement capital restoration plans
A
children.text: is significantly undercapitalized; or

children.label: i
children.text: fails to submit an acceptable capital restoration plan within the time allowed by the appropriate Federal banking agency under subsection (e)(2)(D); or

children.label: ii
children.text: fails in any material respect to implement a plan accepted by the agency.

children.label: i
children.text: Requiring the institution to sell enough shares or obligations of the institution so that the institution will be adequately capitalized after the sale.

children.label: ii
children.text: Further requiring that instruments sold under clause (i) be voting shares.

children.label: iii
children.text: Requiring the institution to be acquired by a depository institution holding company, or to combine with another insured depository institution, if 1 or more grounds exist for appointing a conservator or receiver for the institution.

children.label: i
children.text: Requiring the institution to comply with as if subsection (d)(1) of that section (exempting transactions with certain affiliated institutions) did not apply.section 371c of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Further restricting the institution’s transactions with affiliates.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Restricting the interest rates that the institution pays on deposits to the prevailing rates of interest on deposits of comparable amounts and maturities in the region where the institution is located, as determined by the agency.

children.heading: Retroactive restrictions prohibited
children.label: ii Retroactive restrictions prohibited
children.text: This subparagraph does not authorize the agency to restrict interest rates paid on time deposits made before (and not renewed or renegotiated after) the agency acted under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Restricting asset growth
children.label: D Restricting asset growth
children.text: Restricting the institution’s asset growth more stringently than subsection (e)(3), or requiring the institution to reduce its total assets.

children.heading: Restricting activities
children.label: E Restricting activities
children.text: Requiring the institution or any of its subsidiaries to alter, reduce, or terminate any activity that the agency determines poses excessive risk to the institution.

children.heading: New election of directors
children.label: i New election of directors
children.text: Ordering a new election for the institution’s board of directors.

children.heading: Dismissing directors or senior executive officers
children.label: ii Dismissing directors or senior executive officers
children.text: Requiring the institution to dismiss from office any director or senior executive officer who had held office for more than 180 days immediately before the institution became undercapitalized. Dismissal under this clause shall not be construed to be a removal under .section 1818 of this title

children.heading: Employing qualified senior executive officers
children.label: iii Employing qualified senior executive officers
children.text: Requiring the institution to employ qualified senior executive officers (who, if the agency so specifies, shall be subject to approval by the agency).

children.heading: Prohibiting deposits from correspondent banks
children.label: G Prohibiting deposits from correspondent banks
children.text: Prohibiting the acceptance by the institution of deposits from correspondent depository institutions, including renewals and rollovers of prior deposits.

children.heading: Requiring prior approval for capital distributions by bank holding company
children.label: H Requiring prior approval for capital distributions by bank holding company
children.text: Prohibiting any bank holding company having control of the insured depository institution from making any capital distribution without the prior approval of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.

children.heading: Divestiture by the institution
children.label: i Divestiture by the institution
children.text: Requiring the institution to divest itself of or liquidate any subsidiary if the agency determines that the subsidiary is in danger of becoming insolvent and poses a significant risk to the institution, or is likely to cause a significant dissipation of the institution’s assets or earnings.

children.heading: Divestiture by parent company of nondepository affiliate
children.label: ii Divestiture by parent company of nondepository affiliate
children.text: Requiring any company having control of the institution to divest itself of or liquidate any affiliate other than an insured depository institution if the appropriate Federal banking agency for that company determines that the affiliate is in danger of becoming insolvent and poses a significant risk to the institution, or is likely to cause a significant dissipation of the institution’s assets or earnings.

children.heading: Divestiture of institution
children.label: iii Divestiture of institution
children.text: Requiring any company having control of the institution to divest itself of the institution if the appropriate Federal banking agency for that company determines that divestiture would improve the institution’s financial condition and future prospects.

children.heading: Requiring other action
children.label: J Requiring other action
children.text: Requiring the institution to take any other action that the agency determines will better carry out the purpose of this section than any of the actions described in this paragraph.

children.heading: Presumption in favor of certain actions
children.label: 3 Presumption in favor of certain actions
A
children.text: The action described in clause (i) or (iii) of paragraph (2)(A) (relating to requiring the sale of shares or obligations, or requiring the institution to be acquired by or combine with another institution).

children.label: B
children.text: The action described in paragraph (2)(B)(i) (relating to restricting transactions with affiliates).

children.label: C
children.text: The action described in paragraph (2)(C) (relating to restricting interest rates).

children.label: i
children.text: Pay any bonus to any senior executive officer.

children.label: ii
children.text: Provide compensation to any senior executive officer at a rate exceeding that officer’s average rate of compensation (excluding bonuses, stock options, and profit-sharing) during the 12 calendar months preceding the calendar month in which the institution became undercapitalized.

children.heading: Failing to submit plan
children.label: B Failing to submit plan
children.text: The appropriate Federal banking agency shall not grant any approval under subparagraph (A) with respect to an institution that has failed to submit an acceptable capital restoration plan.

children.heading: Discretion to impose certain additional restrictions
children.label: 5 Discretion to impose certain additional restrictions
children.text: The agency may impose 1 or more of the restrictions prescribed by regulation under subsection (i) if the agency determines that those restrictions are necessary to carry out the purpose of this section.

children.heading: Consultation with other regulators
children.label: 6 Consultation with other regulators
children.text: Before the agency or Corporation makes a determination under paragraph (2)(I) with respect to an affiliate that is a broker, dealer, government securities broker, government securities dealer, investment company, or investment adviser, the agency or Corporation shall consult with the Securities and Exchange Commission and, in the case of any other affiliate which is subject to any financial responsibility or capital requirement, any other appropriate regulator of such affiliate with respect to the proposed determination of the agency or the Corporation and actions pursuant to such determination.

children.heading: More stringent treatment based on other supervisory criteria
children.label: g More stringent treatment based on other supervisory criteria
A
children.text: if the institution is well capitalized, reclassify the institution as adequately capitalized;

children.label: B
children.text: if the institution is adequately capitalized (but not well capitalized), require the institution to comply with 1 or more provisions of subsections (d) and (e), as if the institution were undercapitalized; or

children.label: C
children.text: if the institution is undercapitalized, take any 1 or more actions authorized under subsection (f)(2) as if the institution were significantly undercapitalized.

children.heading: Contents of plan
children.label: 2 Contents of plan
children.text: Any plan required under paragraph (1) shall specify the steps that the insured depository institution will take to correct the unsafe or unsound condition or practice. Capital restoration plans shall not be required under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Provisions applicable to critically undercapitalized institutions
children.label: h Provisions applicable to critically undercapitalized institutions

children.heading: Activities restricted
children.label: 1 Activities restricted
children.text: Any critically undercapitalized insured depository institution shall comply with restrictions prescribed by the Corporation under subsection (i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A critically undercapitalized insured depository institution shall not, beginning 60 days after becoming critically undercapitalized, make any payment of principal or interest on the institution’s subordinated debt.

children.label: i
children.text: the appropriate Federal banking agency has taken action with respect to the insured depository institution under paragraph (3)(A)(ii); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Corporation determines that the exception would further the purpose of this section.

children.heading: Limited exemption for certain subordinated debt
children.label: C Limited exemption for certain subordinated debt
children.text: Until , subparagraph (A) shall not apply with respect to any subordinated debt outstanding on , and not extended or otherwise renegotiated after .July 15, 1996July 15, 1991July 15, 1991

children.heading: Accrual of interest
children.label: D Accrual of interest
children.text: Subparagraph (A) does not prevent unpaid interest from accruing on subordinated debt under the terms of that debt, to the extent otherwise permitted by law.

children.heading: Conservatorship, receivership, or other action required
children.label: 3 Conservatorship, receivership, or other action required
i
children.text: appoint a receiver (or, with the concurrence of the Corporation, a conservator) for the institution; or

children.label: ii
children.text: take such other action as the agency determines, with the concurrence of the Corporation, would better achieve the purpose of this section, after documenting why the action would better achieve that purpose.

children.heading: Periodic redeterminations required
children.label: B Periodic redeterminations required
children.text: Any determination by an appropriate Federal banking agency under subparagraph (A)(ii) to take any action with respect to an insured depository institution in lieu of appointing a conservator or receiver shall cease to be effective not later than the end of the 90-day period beginning on the date that the determination is made and a conservator or receiver shall be appointed for that institution under subparagraph (A)(i) unless the agency makes a new determination under subparagraph (A)(ii) at the end of the effective period of the prior determination.

children.heading: Appointment of receiver required if other action fails to restore capital
children.label: C Appointment of receiver required if other action fails to restore capital

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraphs (A) and (B), the appropriate Federal banking agency shall appoint a receiver for the insured depository institution if the institution is critically undercapitalized on average during the calendar quarter beginning 270 days after the date on which the institution became critically undercapitalized.

children.label: I
children.text: the agency determines, with the concurrence of the Corporation, that (aa) the insured depository institution has positive net worth, (bb) the insured depository institution has been in substantial compliance with an approved capital restoration plan which requires consistent improvement in the institution’s capital since the date of the approval of the plan, (cc) the insured depository institution is profitable or has an upward trend in earnings the agency projects as sustainable, and (dd) the insured depository institution is reducing the ratio of nonperforming loans to total loans; and

children.label: II
children.text: the head of the appropriate Federal banking agency and the Chairperson of the Board of Directors both certify that the institution is viable and not expected to fail.

children.heading: Restricting activities of critically undercapitalized institutions
children.label: i Restricting activities of critically undercapitalized institutions

children.text: restrict the activities of any critically undercapitalized insured depository institution; and

children.label: A
children.text: Entering into any material transaction other than in the usual course of business, including any investment, expansion, acquisition, sale of assets, or other similar action with respect to which the depository institution is required to provide notice to the appropriate Federal banking agency.

children.label: B
children.text: Extending credit for any highly leveraged transaction.

children.label: C
children.text: Amending the institution’s charter or bylaws, except to the extent necessary to carry out any other requirement of any law, regulation, or order.

children.label: D
children.text: Making any material change in accounting methods.

children.label: E
children.text: Engaging in any covered transaction (as defined in ).section 371c(b) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: Paying excessive compensation or bonuses.

children.label: G
children.text: Paying interest on new or renewed liabilities at a rate that would increase the institution’s weighted average cost of funds to a level significantly exceeding the prevailing rates of interest on insured deposits in the institution’s normal market areas.
to an insured depository institution for which the Corporation or the Resolution Trust Corporation is conservator; or
to a bridge depository institution, none of the voting securities of which are owned by a person or agency other than the Corporation or the Resolution Trust Corporation.

children.heading: Reviews required when Deposit Insurance Fund incurs losses
children.label: k Reviews required when Deposit Insurance Fund incurs losses
i
children.text: ascertain why the institution’s problems resulted in a material loss to the Deposit Insurance Fund; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make recommendations for preventing any such loss in the future; and

children.label: i
children.text: the Comptroller General of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Corporation (if the agency is not the Corporation);

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a State depository institution, the appropriate State banking supervisor; and

children.label: iv
children.text: upon request by any Member of Congress, to that Member.

children.label: I
children.text: it is not substantially certain that the assistance will be fully repaid not later than 24 months after the date on which the Corporation initiated the assistance; or

children.label: II
children.text: the institution ceases to repay the assistance in accordance with its terms; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the Corporation is appointed receiver of the institution, and it is or becomes apparent that the present value of the outlays of the Deposit Insurance Fund with respect to that institution will exceed the present value of receivership dividends or other payments on the claims held by the Corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: $200,000,000, if the loss occurs during the period beginning on , and ending on ;January 1, 2010December 31, 2011

children.label: ii
children.text: $150,000,000, if the loss occurs during the period beginning on , and ending on ; andJanuary 1, 2012December 31, 2013

children.label: iii
children.text: $50,000,000, if the loss occurs on or after , provided that if the inspector general of a Federal banking agency certifies to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives that the number of projected failures of depository institutions that would require material loss reviews for the following 12 months will be greater than 30 and would hinder the effectiveness of its oversight functions, then the definition of “material loss” shall be $75,000,000 for a duration of 1 year from the date of the certification.January 1, 2014

children.label: i
children.text: the date on which the institution ceases to repay assistance under in accordance with its terms, orsection 1823(c) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which it becomes apparent that the assistance will not be fully repaid during the 24-month period described in paragraph (2)(A)(i).

children.label: B
children.text: If the institution is described in paragraph (2)(A)(ii), during the 6-month period beginning on the date on which it becomes apparent that the present value of the outlays of the Deposit Insurance Fund with respect to that institution will exceed the present value of receivership dividends or other payments on the claims held by the Corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: any portion under section 552(b)(5) of that title; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any information about the insured depository institution under paragraph (4) (other than trade secrets) or paragraph (8) of section 552(b) of that title.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Subparagraph (A) does not require the agency to disclose the name of any customer of the insured depository institution (other than an institution-affiliated party), or information from which such a person’s identity could reasonably be ascertained.

children.label: i
children.text: identify losses that the Inspector General estimates have been incurred by the Deposit Insurance Fund during that 6-month period, with respect to the insured depository institutions supervised by the Federal banking agency;

children.label: I
children.text: the grounds identified by the Federal banking agency or State bank supervisor for appointing the Corporation as receiver under ; andsection 1821(c)(5) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: whether any unusual circumstances exist that might warrant an in-depth review of the loss; and

children.label: I
children.text: an identification of any loss that warrants an in-depth review, together with the reasons why such review is warranted, or, if the Inspector General determines that no review is warranted, an explanation of such determination; and

children.label: II
children.text: for each loss identified under subclause (I) that warrants an in-depth review, the date by which such review, and a report on such review prepared in a manner consistent with reports under paragraph (1)(A), will be completed and submitted to the Federal banking agency and Congress.

children.label: i
children.text: submit each report required under paragraph (A) expeditiously, and not later than 90 days after the end of the 6-month period covered by the report; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide a copy of the report required under paragraph (A) to any Member of Congress, upon request.

children.heading: GAO review
children.label: 6 GAO review
children.text: The Comptroller General of the United States shall, under such conditions as the Comptroller General determines to be appropriate, review reports made under paragraph (1) and recommend improvements in the supervision of insured depository institutions (including the implementation of this section).

children.heading: Regulations and other actions
children.label: 1 Regulations and other actions
children.text: Each appropriate Federal banking agency shall prescribe such regulations (in consultation with the other Federal banking agencies), issue such orders, and take such other actions as are necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Written determination and concurrence required
children.label: 2 Written determination and concurrence required
children.text: Any determination or concurrence by an appropriate Federal banking agency or the Corporation required under this section shall be written.

children.heading: Other authority not affected
children.label: m Other authority not affected
children.text: This section does not limit any authority of an appropriate Federal banking agency, the Corporation, or a State to take action in addition to (but not in derogation of) that required under this section.

children.heading: Administrative review of dismissal orders
children.label: n Administrative review of dismissal orders

children.heading: Timely petition required
children.label: 1 Timely petition required
children.text: A director or senior executive officer dismissed pursuant to an order under subsection (f)(2)(F)(ii) may obtain review of that order by filing a written petition for reinstatement with the appropriate Federal banking agency not later than 10 days after receiving notice of the dismissal.

children.label: i
children.text: submit written materials in support of the petition; and

children.label: ii
children.text: appear, personally or through counsel, before 1 or more members of the agency or designated employees of the agency.

children.label: i
children.text: schedule the hearing referred to in subparagraph (A)(ii) promptly after the petition is filed; and

children.label: ii
children.text: hold the hearing not later than 30 days after the petition is filed, unless the petitioner requests that the hearing be held at a later time.

children.label: i
children.text: by order, grant or deny the petition;

children.label: ii
children.text: if the order is adverse to the petitioner, set forth the basis for the order; and

children.label: iii
children.text: notify the petitioner of the order.

children.heading: Standard for review of dismissal orders
children.label: 3 Standard for review of dismissal orders
A
children.text: to become adequately capitalized, to the extent that the order is based on the institution’s capital level or failure to submit or implement a capital restoration plan; and

children.label: B
children.text: to correct the unsafe or unsound condition or unsafe or unsound practice, to the extent that the order is based on subsection (g)(1).

children.heading: Transition rules for savings associations
children.label: o Transition rules for savings associations
A
children.text: the savings association had submitted a plan meeting the requirements of ; andsection 1464(t)(6)(A)(ii) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the Director of the Office of Thrift Supervision had accepted the plan;
the plan remains in effect; and
the savings association remains in compliance with the plan or is operating under a written agreement with the appropriate Federal banking agency.
